reasoning: The Lean statement encodes exactly the stars-and-bars counting problem from the informal text. Each function `f : Fin 6 → ℕ` represents an assignment of ball-counts to the 6 distinguishable sons (indexed by `Fin 6`), with `f i` being the number of identical balls son i receives. The condition `∀ i, f i > 0` faithfully encodes "everyone gets at least one", and `∑ i, f i = 10` encodes that all 10 balls are distributed. The set `sols` is defined by an iff specification to be exactly the set of such distributions, and `sols.card` counts them. The treatment of sons as distinguishable (via indexing) and balls as identical (just numerical counts) matches the informal problem precisely. No hypotheses are added or omitted.
